text.title: Freelancer Personal CRM (Minimal) Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding Freelancer Personal CRM (Minimal). Each pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale; HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override.

rule.statement: Six stages: lead / qualified / proposal / signed / active / churned. Extra stages = sales-funnel theatre.
rule.rationale: Six stages capture the freelancer pipeline without inventing imaginary milestones.

rule.statement: ≤12 fields per record: name, contact, source, stage, last_touch, next_touch, value_estimate, status_note, tags, owner, created_at, archived.
rule.rationale: More fields = data not maintained; ≤12 stays fillable in <30 sec/record.

rule.statement: Weekly 30-min review: every record touched in last 14 days OR moved/archived.
rule.rationale: Without weekly touch, records rot; rotting CRM is no CRM.

rule.statement: Stage transitions require a note (≥1 sentence). 'Lead → Qualified' silent = sales fiction.
rule.rationale: Notes prevent stage inflation; force the freelancer to articulate why the prospect moved.

rule.statement: Quarterly: archive records with last_touch >90 days AND stage in [lead, qualified, churned].
rule.rationale: Stale records distort pipeline value + waste review cycles.

rule.statement: If preconditions in AGENTS.md "Applies If" do not all hold, OR any "Skip If" disqualifier is true, do not run this methodology — route to the named alternative.
rule.rationale: Methodologies are pre-condition gated; running them out-of-scope produces noise and burns the agent context budget.

rule.statement: If preconditions hold, execute the procedure in `content/04-procedure.xml` (or the rule set above when complexity is light), emit the artefact specified by `content/02-output-contract.xml`, and validate with `scripts/validate-freelancer-personal-crm-minimal.py` before returning.
rule.rationale: Tying the entry rule to the validator script closes the loop between policy and machine check.
